chunks.text: HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Application No.S-723 of 2023 [Nazar Muhammad @ Popat & Others versus The State] Applicants: Through Mr. Waheed Ahmed Awan advocate Complainant: Through Mr. Babar Hussain Arain advocate State: Through Mr. Siraj A. Bijarani Assistant P.G Date of hearing: 08.09.2023 Date of Order: 08.09.2023 O R D E R MAHMOOD A. KHAN J:- Applicants seek interim pre-arrest bail in Crime No.27 of 2023 registered at P.S 60th Mile for offences punishable under Sections 506, 504, 337-L(ii) and 34 PPC, after rejection of their plea for the same relief by learned trial Court vide Order dated 12.07.2023. 2- Facts of the FIR find sufficient elaborate in the memo of bail application as well as impugned Order, hence there is no need to reproduce the same for the sake of brevity. However, the allegation against the applicants, per FIR, is that on 08.06.2023 at about 2030 hours they had caused injuries by hatchet and lathies to complainant party on account of alleged dispute over landed property. 3- At the very outset, on being confronted with the available medical certificate, learned Assistant P.G assisted by learned counsel for the Complainant frankly conceded for confirmation of bail keeping in view the fact that as per medical certificate the injuries fall within the ambit of other hurts, hence same are not of grievous in nature; however, learned counsel for the Complainant submits that trial Court may be directed to conclude the trial within shortest possible time in accordance with law. 4- In view of the above position of the case, the captioned bail application is allowed.

KHAN J:- Applicants seek interim pre-arrest bail in Crime No.27 of 2023 registered at P.S 60th Mile for offences punishable under Sections 506, 504, 337-L(ii) and 34 PPC, after rejection of their plea for the same relief by learned trial Court vide Order dated 12.07.2023. 2- Facts of the FIR find sufficient elaborate in the memo of bail application as well as impugned Order, hence there is no need to reproduce the same for the sake of brevity. However, the allegation against the applicants, per FIR, is that on 08.06.2023 at about 2030 hours they had caused injuries by hatchet and lathies to complainant party on account of alleged dispute over landed property. 3- At the very outset, on being confronted with the available medical certificate, learned Assistant P.G assisted by learned counsel for the Complainant frankly conceded for confirmation of bail keeping in view the fact that as per medical certificate the injuries fall within the ambit of other hurts, hence same are not of grievous in nature; however, learned counsel for the Complainant submits that trial Court may be directed to conclude the trial within shortest possible time in accordance with law. 4- In view of the above position of the case, the captioned bail application is allowed. Consequently the interim pre-arrest bail granted to the applicants, vide Order dated 14.07.2023, is confirmed on same terms and conditions. However, the learned trial Court is directed to make a sincere attempt for conclusion of the trial in accordance with law within a period of three months from today. It is also observed that learned trial Court shall be competent to act in accordance with law, without making any reference to this Court, in case applicants misuse the concession of bail at any stage. Captioned bail application stands disposed of accordingly. JUDGE Sajjad Ali Jessar
